title: Ben Lerner Hears Ghosts in the Wires
summary: In a short story called “Wireless,” published in Scribner’s in 1901, Rudyard Kipling described “a glass tube” with “two tiny silver plugs, and between them an infinitesimal pinch of metallic dust.” Fiddling with the plugs, Kipling’s protagonist slips into a trance and spontaneously transcribes John Keats’s “The Eve of St. Agnes,” a poem he has […]
reason: Literary criticism about mediation and transmission is interesting but operates entirely in the representational register with no routing-level implications.

title: Capitalist Profits Depend on Stealing Our Future
summary: We live in an age in which extraordinary wealth seems to arrive from unfathomable sources. When the US firm Uber went public in 2019, the stock market set its value at $82 billion, an immense figure for a ten-year-old car service company that owned no cars and had never made a profit. To explain such […]
reason: The Uber example concretely illustrates how platform capital extracts value from commons (distributed labor, public infrastructure, social trust) it cannot produce itself, making the capture mechanism visible.
angle: Use the 'alibi structure' tool to show how market valuation mystifies the actual source of platform value—unpaid commons labor and infrastructural subsidy—and what becomes visible when that alibi is named.

title: Serbia’s Government Is Targeting the Public University
summary: In the seventeenth month of a student-led mass movement in Serbia, the death of a student at the Faculty of Philosophy triggered an orchestrated attack on the University of Belgrade by the country’s government-police-tabloid complex. On the eve of the police raid, protesters gathered in front of the rectorate, and the rector delivered a historic […]
reason: A student-led mass movement defending a public university from state-police-media enclosure is a textbook case of the commune layer under direct attack, with the institution itself becoming a routing node for distributed resistance.
angle: Use the routing architecture tool: the university is not just a symbol but an actual infrastructure through which care, knowledge, and solidarity are being routed outside state and market control — which is precisely why it is being targeted.

title: The Oldest Octopus Fossil Ever Isn’t An Octopus At All, Scientists Discover
summary: A re-examination of a 300-million-year-old fossil that was long thought to be the earliest octopus revealed that the animal was actually part of the nautilus family.
